note: Advisory only. This map surfaces compelled-disclosure exposure — which government can compel disclosure of a flow's data via the provider's home legal regime (e.g. US CLOUD Act, PRC DSL/CSL/PIPL). It does NOT adjudicate whether a given statute legally applies to any specific flow; the policy decides. Sovereignty is distinct from residency (where the endpoint physically sits).

sources.us: CLOUD Act 2018, FISA 702, Patriot Act §215 — US-headquartered providers subject to US compelled-disclosure process regardless of endpoint region.
sources.eu: EU/EEA member-state privacy + data-protection regimes; compelled disclosure routes through the member state but the bloc is the meaningful policy unit.
sources.cn: PRC Data Security Law, Cybersecurity Law, PIPL — broad disclosure obligations to PRC authorities.
sources.uk: Investigatory Powers Act 2016 — post-Brexit distinct from EU.
sources.ru: SORM; FSB access under Russian law.
sources.il: Israeli privacy law and surveillance framework.
sources.ca: PIPEDA + the CSIS/RCMP production-order regime — Canadian-headquartered providers are subject to Canadian compelled-disclosure process.
sources.local: Self-hosted inference — no external sovereign; the operator's own jurisdiction governs.
sources.unknown: Sovereignty not statically determinable (aggregator/router or unmapped host).
sources.jp: APPI (Act on the Protection of Personal Information) + Criminal Procedure Code access — Japanese-headquartered providers subject to Japanese compelled-disclosure process.
sources.kr: PIPA 2011 + Protection of Communications Secrets Act — Korean-headquartered providers subject to Korean compelled-disclosure process.
sources.sg: PDPA 2012 + Criminal Procedure Code 2010 / Computer Misuse Act — Singapore-headquartered providers subject to Singaporean compelled-disclosure process.
sources.au: TOLA 2018 (Assistance and Access Act) + Privacy Act 1988 — Australian-headquartered providers subject to Australian compelled-disclosure and technical-assistance process.
sources.ae: Federal Decree-Law 45/2021 (PDPL) + broad state-security access — UAE-headquartered providers subject to Emirati compelled-disclosure process.
sources.ch: Revised FADP (in force 2023) + BÜPF lawful-interception framework — Swiss-headquartered providers subject to Swiss compelled-disclosure process; Switzerland is outside the EU/EEA.
